what_it_does: This file is a metadata-only attachment record that links a channel-memory entry to an image asset in the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter with no body content
Fields: id att-b1acc35a, channel design-source, attachment_type images, target image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-07, added 2026-06-22T11:31:01.433758+00:00
Uses an image:// URI scheme for the target
standouts: References a specific presentation slide image p-07 under design-source/presentation-15p
Typed explicitly as an images attachment
Serves as a cross-reference ledger entry rather than storing the image itself
concerns: No caption, title, or description for the image
No author or provenance beyond a timestamp
Target URI validity cannot be checked from this file alone
notes: A bare attachment ledger card that points the design-source channel at presentation slide image p-07.
questions: What content is shown in presentation-15p/p-07?
Who or what process added this attachment?
How is the image:// target resolved and verified?
What channel-memory entry does this attachment belong to?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file declares an image attachment and points to the intended image target.
intention_signals: To catalog and link a specific presentation slide image into the design-source channel memory so visual design material can be referenced by the ledger.
novelty: Uses an image:// target scheme and an explicit attachment_type taxonomy to separate image assets from channel content.
contribution: Provides a stable, hash-identified attachment record that maps the design-source channel to a presentation image asset, supporting image retrieval and provenance without embedding binary data.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record (att-b1acc35a) in the design-source channel that references the image target image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-07 with type images and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to catalog and link a specific presentation slide image into the design-source channel memory.
